questions.question_text: Why is marketing claiming 3x ROAS when the CFO's model shows 1.4x?
questions.answer_summary: Marketing attributes conversions within a 28-day click window and counts view-through conversions. Finance uses a 7-day last-click model. The 28-day window double-counts customers who would have purchased organically, inflating ROAS by 2.1x.

questions.question_text: Are our inventory turn numbers hiding slow-moving SKUs?
questions.answer_summary: The blended inventory turn rate of 8.2x masks 340 SKUs with turns below 2x. These slow movers tie up $3.4M in working capital and aren't flagged because the metric averages across all categories.

questions.question_text: Is our customer lifetime value calculation actually predictive?
questions.answer_summary: LTV uses a 3-year horizon but the model was trained on pre-2022 purchase patterns. Post-inflation buying behavior has shortened repeat cycles by 18%, making the current $420 LTV estimate roughly $85 too high.

questions.question_text: Did last quarter's promotion actually drive incremental revenue?
questions.answer_summary: The promo report shows $2.8M in attributed revenue, but 61% of those transactions were from loyalty members who purchased at full price in prior periods. Incremental lift was closer to $1.1M after accounting for pull-forward effects.

questions.question_text: How reliable is our channel mix model for next year's budget?
questions.answer_summary: The model treats in-store and online as independent channels, but 38% of online orders involve store-level fulfillment costs that aren't allocated back. E-commerce margin is overstated by 4.2 points in the planning model.
